intro: The clearest difference between Checkmarx and SonarQube is pricing — Checkmarx is unknown (no free tier), while SonarQube is freemium (has a free tier). Beyond that, lock-in — Checkmarx has high lock-in (Medium-High — enterprise SAST, deep integration), whereas SonarQube has low lock-in (OSS Community edition available). The stated fit for both tools is similar on paper: Checkmarx is pitched as enterprise SAST, SCA, IaC, and API security scanning platform covering 30+ languages with IDE and CI integration, while SonarQube is pitched as SAST and code quality platform detecting security vulnerabilities, bugs, and code smells across 30+ programming languages. On the strengths side, Checkmarx is cited for UGC platforms (social, marketplace), while SonarQube is cited for Chat/messaging apps. The honest trade-off: both Checkmarx and SonarQube are the wrong call for internal projects. Choosing between them should come down to the constraint that actually binds your workflow, not surface-level overlap. On developer experience the data shows Checkmarx at 3/5 and SonarQube at 5/5, with transparency at 1/5 and 4/5 respectively.
quick_take: Checkmarx and SonarQube both target Content Moderation API; their data-level fit descriptions overlap, so decide based on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose Checkmarx if you need a Content Moderation API tool and a unknown (no free tier) pricing shape fits your budget better than freemium (has a free tier).
verdict_b: Choose SonarQube if you need a Content Moderation API tool and a freemium (has a free tier) pricing shape fits your budget better than unknown (no free tier).

faq.a: Checkmarx is unknown, while SonarQube is freemium with a free tier, so the cheaper option depends on your usage shape. Listed pricing pages usually change, so check both directly before committing. For small teams, the free-tier limits often matter more than headline prices.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Checkmarx to SonarQube?
faq.a: Migrating from Checkmarx to SonarQube is feasible when their scopes overlap, but the work is in re-mapping configuration, data models, and integrations. Moving off Checkmarx is harder because of high lock-in (Medium-High — enterprise SAST, deep integration). Plan for a dual-run period so you can validate parity before decommissioning.

On our developer-experience score, SonarQube is rated 5/5 and Checkmarx 3/5, so SonarQube edges ahead. Scores like this smooth over real differences in docs, CLI feel, and SDK quality, so treat them as a starting point. Spend an afternoon in each before relying on the number.

faq.q: Is SonarQube a good alternative to Checkmarx?
faq.a: SonarQube is a reasonable alternative to Checkmarx when SonarQube fits marketplace, apps. If your use case matches Checkmarx's sweet spot — marketplace, apps — staying with Checkmarx is probably safer. Checkmarx itself is not meant for internal projects, which is often where users start shopping for alternatives.
